rationale: Baseline experiment replicating Dahiyat & Mayo 1997 original method. Minimal rotamer sampling, core-only design. Expect fast runtime and moderate energy score. This establishes the "lean" end of the spectrum.

Baseline: Dahiyat & Mayo 1997 style (no extra sampling, no input SC). Establishes fastest runtime and baseline energy. Small 36-residue protein makes this feasible. Expect moderate score but minimal runtime.
Modern default (ex1+ex2 sampling with input SC). Most comprehensive search space. Expect best quality score but slowest runtime. Provides upper bound for score quality.
Light extra sampling: ex1 only without input SC. Tests if chi1 rotamer expansion alone improves quality over baseline. Should be moderately faster than Run 2.
Isolate input SC benefit: standard rotamers but with input sidechain. Tests if good input structure alone (without extra sampling) improves both score and runtime vs baseline.
Combining ex1 + input_sc (Run 3 showed ex1 helps, Run 4 showed input_sc helps). Should achieve good score with better runtime than Run 3. Hypothesis: this could be the sweet spot.
Test aromatic sampling with input_sc. ex1aro=True might help with aromatic packing without full ex1 overhead. Could offer different quality/speed trade-off.
Combine ex1 + ex1aro + input_sc. Tests if both chi1 types of sampling with input SC can beat our best so far, and at what runtime cost.
Test ex2 alone with input_sc (skip ex1). Chi2 sampling without chi1 is uncommon but worth testing as final exploration to see if it offers better trade-off than ex1.

Modern Rosetta default configuration with maximal rotamer sampling (ex1+ex2) and design of all residues. Expect slower runtime but potentially better energy scores. Establishes the "rich" end of the spectrum.
Moderate rotamer sampling (ex1 only) with all residues + input sidechain. This should offer a good speed/quality trade-off. Hypothesis: better speed than Run 2 with minimal score loss.
Core design with ex1 sampling but no input sidechain. Tests whether ex1 helps core-focused design and whether input_sc is necessary. Should be faster than Run 3 while exploring core's efficiency.

Test whether input_sc is actually helping in Run 3. If removing it doesn't hurt the score much but saves rotamers/time, it's a better configuration. Hypothesis: input_sc may not be critical for good design when using ex1.

Add ex1aro to Run 3 to see if aromatic packing improves the score. May provide better side-chain orientations for packing-critical aromatics. Could slightly increase runtime but potentially improve final score.

Minimal sampling (no ex1/ex2) but with all residues and input_sc. Tests if input_sc alone without extra rotamers can achieve good scores. Should be very fast - a minimal baseline for all-residue design.

Maximum rotamer sampling (ex1+ex2+ex1aro). Test if adding ex1aro to Run 2 improves score beyond -161.192. Final exploration of the "maximum quality" regime. Expect slowest runtime but potentially best score.

Baseline run with minimal rotamer sampling (20 residues total). This matches Dahiyat & Mayo's original spirit but with designable_region='all' (appropriate for small proteins). Will establish runtime and energy baseline. Expect moderate runtime (~20-30s) and reasonable energy score.

Add ex1 sampling to baseline. This tests whether extra chi1 rotamers (+/-1 SD) improve final energy score while remaining computationally efficient. For a small 20-residue protein, the rotamer count increase should be modest. Expect slightly lower score (better) and ~15-20s runtime.

Add ex1 + use_input_sc. Including the input sidechain can improve packing if the input structure is high-quality (1L2Y should be). This is a modest modification that respects structural information. Expect good energy score and moderate runtime.

Full modern Rosetta default (ex1=True, ex2=True, use_input_sc=True). This is the most comprehensive rotamer sampling with input structure info. Expect the best energy score but will be slower. For a 20-residue protein, should still be <60s. This is our upper bound on quality.

Test ex1 + ex1aro sampling. This focuses extra rotamer sampling on aromatic residues (which may be abundant in 1L2Y's core). Less expensive than full ex2, but targets critical packing interactions. Expect moderate runtime and potentially good energy if aromatics are important.

Test use_input_sc as the ONLY modification to baseline. Since ex1+use_input_sc gave -35.034 with same runtime as baseline+ex1, this isolates whether use_input_sc alone (without ex1) can achieve that improvement. If it does, we have a minimal optimal configuration.
Test ex2 alone without ex1. Since ex1 alone gave no benefit over baseline, does ex2 help? This tests whether higher-order chi angles are important for this protein's packing.

Most aggressive configuration (all flags on). This is an upper bound on rotamer sampling. If this doesn't beat ex1+ex2+use_input_sc, it suggests diminishing returns. Expect slowest runtime but may reveal if even finer sampling helps.

Test ex2 + ex1aro + use_input_sc (without ex1). This tests whether focusing on ex2 (chi2 angles) and aromatic rotamers while using input SC can match the best result without ex1. May reveal an alternative minimal configuration.
